description:
Offered: Spring
This course equips students with skills in quantitative and qualitative research methods. Students will learn how to design and apply methods such as surveys, interviews, and content analysis to investigate social issues, while also gaining handson experience in literature reviews, data collection, and data interpretation.
Prerequisite(s): SOC 1100 and one from MATH 1100 , MATH 1700 , MATH 1800 . Sophomore, junior, or senior status.
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, IHIP
prerequisites: SOC 1100 and one from MATH 1100 , MATH 1700 , MATH 1800 . Sophomore, junior, or senior status.
